status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

notes: Refactored 2026-06-25 from a topic-numbered feature list (pipeline-001/parsing-001/parsing-002/templates-001/generation-001/generation-002/integrity-001/compile-001/knowledge_base-001) to track core engine functionality components instead, per explicit user instruction: 'Remove all data-specific topic numbers... Replace them with core engine functionality components.' Breadth-of-topics-covered (e.g. '6 of 10 syllabus topics parsed') is intentionally no longer tracked here -- it is content-completeness, not engine functionality. Evidence below is honestly carried over from the prior features' real verification history, not re-asserted from scratch. 2026-06-26: architecture upgraded to local-first deterministic pipeline (FEAT-006/007/008); FEAT-003 multi-source stitching deferred until new pipeline is established.

features.title: Mechanical Parser Pipeline
features.user_visible_behavior: Scripts in 02_parsers/ extract raw sources (01_raw_sources/) into clean, topic-grouped, living Markdown in 03_knowledge_base/ -- both class-notes (PDF/DOCX) and past-paper QP/MS pairs.
verification: Run parse_class_notes.py against a class-notes PDF and DOCX; confirm clean Topic_XX_Name.md output with no leftover formatting artifacts.
Run parse_past_paper.py against an official QP/MS pair; confirm each question part is correctly paired with its mark scheme entry.
Confirm re-running against a new source for an existing topic appends/merges rather than destructively overwriting (Living Database).
evidence: parse_class_notes.py dispatches PDF (pdfplumber heuristics) vs DOCX (real structural signals: all-bold run = heading, Word <w:numPr> = bullet, iter_inner_content() for true document order). Appends to an existing Topic_XX_Name.md as a new section rather than overwriting, so a topic's Markdown accumulates from multiple raw sources.

parse_past_paper.py links QP to MS by question-part label. MS side reads CIE's real bordered Question/Answer/Marks table directly via pdfplumber.find_tables() (column-position-independent, unlike an earlier hardcoded-x-threshold approach that silently produced zero matches on some papers); QP side peels chained inline sub-part markers and guards against zero-padded pseudocode line-number listings hijacking the main-question counter.

Verified against the 2020 gold-standard paper (0478_s20_11: 41 parts, 29 matched) plus all 12 complete 2025 QP/MS pairs across both Paper 1 (theory) and Paper 2 (programming) formats -- Paper 2 match rate improved from an initial 16% to a 70-92% range after root-causing real parser bugs (column-position fragility, a false-positive boilerplate filter dropping bare-numeric labels, multi-page answer chunks overwriting each other instead of accumulating).

Verified against 6 class-notes topics' worth of PDF+DOCX sources; two real DOCX heading-detection bugs found and fixed (a missing-whitespace numbering gap, and a length-cap rejecting an otherwise-correctly-bold heading).

NOTE: parse_class_notes.py, parse_past_paper.py, route_questions_ollama.py, route_questions.py, and merge_theory_ollama.py deprecated 2026-06-26 and replaced by FEAT-006/007/008 (extract_docling / link_qa / route_hybrid). Evidence above retained for historical fidelity.
features.notes: Superseded by FEAT-006 (Docling extraction), FEAT-007 (Regex linker), FEAT-008 (Hybrid router). Old heuristic parsers deleted.

features.title: Syllabus Anchor & Mapping Logic
features.user_visible_behavior: Given a generic input prompt or a study plan, the agent maps the requested competencies against the official syllabus in 00_syllabus/ before pulling and filtering content from 03_knowledge_base/, so terminology/scope/depth always matches Cambridge 0478 -- never the reverse.
verification: Given a prompt naming a topic/sub-topic, confirm the agent reads 00_syllabus/ first to confirm exact scope and official terminology before touching 03_knowledge_base/.
Confirm any out-of-scope or non-syllabus-terminology content found in the knowledge base gets filtered/corrected to match the syllabus in the generated output, not the reverse.
evidence: Demonstrated end-to-end during the Topic 2 (Data Transmission) generation pass: read 00_syllabus/Cambridge_0478_Syllabus_2026-2028.pdf's Topic 2 section first to confirm exact subtopic scope (2.1/2.2/2.3) and official terminology before pulling any 03_knowledge_base/ content.

This cross-reference caught a real terminology mismatch: the syllabus says 'automatic repeat query (ARQ)', but the knowledge-base file said 'Automatic Repeat reQuest (ARQ)' (correct-sounding but wrong for this syllabus). Fixed in both the knowledge base (Living Database refinement) and the generated output -- the syllabus's terminology won, not the source material's.

Demonstrated for the prompt-driven input path (a direct topic request). The study-plan-file input path now has a real artifact to exercise it against: 00_syllabus/6_month_zero_to_hero_plan.md (24-week curriculum table mapping each week's Focus to a suggested Topic_XX_Name.md), authored 2026-06-25 from a maintainer-supplied document. Generation against a specific week of this plan has not yet been run -- the mapping column is a convenience reference only and still requires the syllabus-PDF cross-check at generation time per RULE 3.
features.notes: Mapping is currently performed by the agent reading the syllabus PDF directly each time, not via a separate reusable lookup artifact. That remains sufficient verification per this feature's stated behavior (read syllabus -> map -> extract), which does not mandate a specific implementation mechanism.

features.title: High-Fidelity LaTeX Compiler
features.user_visible_behavior: The agent stitches one or more 03_knowledge_base/ Markdown inputs into a structured .tex file using only the structural environments defined in 04_templates/, producing a syntactically valid .tex as the production deliverable (PDF delivery out of scope).
verification: Generate a .tex from a single knowledge-base topic file + the blueprint; confirm only stratbox/critbox/scenario/template-defined structure is used and every claim traces to the source Markdown.

Generate a .tex stitching MULTIPLE knowledge-base files (e.g. several topics, or a topic's notes + its linked past-paper Markdown) into one structurally-agnostic document (quiz/bootcamp/worksheet/etc.).
./compile.sh reports PASS (exit code 0) for the generated file.
evidence: Single-topic stitching fully verified: 05_output/Topic_01_Data_Representation.tex (hand-authored, pipeline-001-era) and 05_output/Topic_02_Data_Transmission.tex (authored via this feature's process) both pass ./compile.sh with zero errors, use only stratbox/critbox/scenario, and every claim traces to their respective 03_knowledge_base/ files with no hallucinated content. Boxes are used conditionally -- stratbox was correctly omitted from Topic 2 since its source has no exam-strategy-style tip, rather than forcing one in.

04_templates/igcse_preamble.tex (the single shared blueprint: stratbox/critbox/scenario via tcolorbox, SenpaiCorner house style) and igcse_blueprint.tex (structural demo + documented class-notes and worksheet skeletons) both verified compiling via ./compile.sh after being copied into 05_output/ for the formal pass.

Extended 2026-06-25 per explicit user instruction: merged three more environments (learningoutcomes, errortrap, modelans) into igcse_preamble.tex from a maintainer-supplied reference document, recoloring errortrap to orange (the source document's errortrap collided visually with critbox's existing red). Added a documented 'weekly tutoring document' skeleton to igcse_blueprint.tex (Learning Objectives -> Comprehensive Notes -> In-Class Worksheet -> Homework -> newpage -> Mark Scheme) plus longtable/tikz packages needed by it. Re-verified via ./compile.sh igcse_blueprint.tex -> PASS with all six boxes rendering.

Multi-topic / multi-source stitching (combining several Topic_XX_Name.md files into one document, or a topic's notes with its linked PastPaper_<code>.md into a worksheet+mark-scheme document) has NOT yet been attempted -- this is the active gap.
DEFERRED 2026-06-26: multi-source stitching suspended while FEAT-006/007/008 pipeline rewrite is active. Will resume once new pipeline is verified.

Session 017 (2026-06-29): Regenerated 05_output/Topic_01_Data_Representation.tex from scratch using 03_knowledge_base/Topic_01_Data_Representation/ (Theory.md + Syllabus.md primary; Past_Papers.md consulted for exam-tip and mark-scheme patterns). All 6 custom environments used: learningoutcomes (scope block), critbox x4 (why-binary misconception, overflow 2-mark requirement, MSB/LSB reversal, Unicode variable-length), stratbox x3 (show working tip, compare ASCII/Unicode, drawback chain), errortrap x3 (carry row in addition, lost 1-bit in shifts, 1024 vs 1000), scenario x6 (conversion worked examples, binary addition, two's complement, file size calculations, RLE), modelans x1 (two's complement mark scheme). Zero hallucinated content. ./compile.sh PASS exit 0. Root cause of two compile failures recorded: (1) parentheses in tcolorbox optional titles are pgfkeys-parsed -- fixed by removing from title text; (2) commas in tcolorbox optional titles are pgfkeys key separators -- fixed by removing commas from all custom titles.

Session 018 (2026-06-29): Generated 05_output/Topic_02_Data_Transmission.tex from scratch using 03_knowledge_base/Topic_02_Data_Transmission/ (Theory.md + Syllabus.md; Past_Papers.md consulted for exam-tip and scenario patterns). Covers syllabus 2.1 (packets, packet switching, simplex/half-duplex/full-duplex, serial/parallel, six combined types, USB) + 2.2 (parity check, parity block, checksum, echo check, check digit, ARQ) + 2.3 (symmetric, asymmetric, key creation). All 6 custom environments used: learningoutcomes (scope block at top), stratbox x4 (packet structure tip, serial-vs-parallel decision guide, parity-block advantage, symmetric-vs-asymmetric comparison table), critbox x5 (simplex printer limitation, parity check limitations, echo check drawback, check digit vs checksum, packet payload vs header), errortrap x1 (corruption definition), scenario x3 (annotating packet-switching diagram, choosing serial simplex for theatre scenario, HTTPS asymmetric encryption), modelans x1 (parity and ARQ combined mark-scheme guidance). Zero hallucinated content -- all claims trace to Theory.md or Past_Papers.md mark-scheme entries. ./compile.sh Topic_02_Data_Transmission.tex -> PASS exit 0.

Covers syllabus 4.1 (system vs application software, OS functions, software hierarchy Hardware->Firmware->OS->Apps, interrupt types and handling cycle, ISR) + 4.2 (high-level vs low-level languages, assembler/compiler/interpreter, IDE functions: code editor/run-time environment/error diagnostics/auto-completion/auto-correction/prettyprint). Three TikZ diagrams: URL component breakdown, DNS resolution triangle, DDoS botnet attack. Session 021 (2026-06-30): Generated 05_output/Topic_10_Boolean_Logic.tex from scratch using 03_knowledge_base/Topic_10_Boolean_Logic/ (Theory.md + Syllabus.md). Covers syllabus 10.1/10.2 (NOT/AND/OR/NAND/NOR/XOR: expressions, truth tables, function descriptions, gate symbols) + 10.3 (creating circuits from problem statements/expressions/truth tables; completing truth tables with intermediate columns; writing expressions from circuits/statements/truth tables). All 6 custom environments used: learningoutcomes (scope block at top), critbox x2 (NAND/NOR full-inverse rule, missing brackets in expressions), stratbox x2 (gate output memory guide, show intermediate columns in exam), errortrap x2 (XOR (1,1) row trap, missing rows in truth table), scenario x2 (circuit from expression, expression from problem statement), modelans x1 (writing expression from truth table -- two-row Sum of Products worked example). Two circuitikz diagrams: 2x3 gate reference grid (NOT/AND/OR/NAND/NOR/XOR with ANSI port shapes) and wired circuit for X=(A AND B) OR (NOT C). circuitikz installed via tlmgr; added to igcse_preamble.tex. First compile failed (circuitikz not installed); second compile -> PASS exit 0 (5 pages, 98539 bytes).
features.notes: Single-topic stitching re-verified with all 6 environments. Multi-source stitching (combining multiple topic files or a full worksheet from Past_Papers.md) remains the outstanding gap for full PASS status.

features.title: Context-Compression Engine
features.user_visible_behavior: When claude-progress.md (or any operational log) nears its size limit, past session history is rolled into a single dense summary block rather than deleted, freeing context budget while preserving high-signal state.
verification: Confirm an operational log that has grown across multiple sessions gets compressed into one summary paragraph for older sessions, keeping only the most recent 1-2 sessions expanded.
Confirm no load-bearing state (current blocker, next step, key fixed bugs) is lost in the compression.
evidence: First executed in an earlier session: Session 001's full log entry was rolled into a single 'Past Context' summary line when claude-progress.md grew past its first multi-session point, per CLAUDE.md's RULE 5 (Context Compression & Rolling Summaries).

Executed again 2026-06-25 on explicit instruction: Sessions 001-005 (the prior 'Past Context' line plus 4 full expanded session entries) were rolled into one dense summary paragraph, keeping claude-progress.md's 'Current Verified State' header (which already tracks current blocker/next step independently) intact and unsummarized.
features.notes: This is a manual editorial process the agent performs per CLAUDE.md RULE 5 on review or explicit instruction, not an automated script -- 'engine' here means a reliably-followed rule, not literal code. That is sufficient per this feature's stated user-visible behavior, which does not mandate a specific implementation mechanism.

features.title: Semantic Topic Tagger (Past Papers -> Topical KB)
features.user_visible_behavior: A Python script (02_parsers/route_questions_ollama.py) reads PastPaper_<code>.md files, groups sub-parts by main question number, sends each group to a local Ollama LLM for topic classification, and appends the Q&A pair (with RULE 10 YAML frontmatter: breadcrumbs/source_file/total_marks/tags) to the correct 03_knowledge_base/<Topic_XX_Name>/Past_Papers.md.
verification: Run route_questions_ollama.py against all PastPaper_*.md files.
Verify 03_knowledge_base/ contains exactly 10 canonical Topic_XX_Name/ folders.
Verify each Past_Papers.md receiving content has YAML frontmatter blocks (breadcrumbs/source_file/total_marks/tags).
Verify no mark schemes were detached from their questions during routing.
evidence: 02_parsers/split_syllabus.py (new, deterministic): parsed 00_syllabus/keyword_routing.md on '### Topic N: Name' headers and wrote Syllabus.md into each of the 10 canonical topic folders. Verified: all 10 folders now have Syllabus.md.

02_parsers/route_questions_ollama.py (new, FEAT-005): runs against all PastPaper_*.md files in batch mode (auto-glob). Groups sub-parts by main question number before sending to Ollama (avoids splitting a single scenario across calls). Key robustness fixes: json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode() for LLM trailing prose, VALID_TOPIC_FOLDERS guardrail (strict match + case-insensitive fallback) to prevent hallucinated directory creation, Windows-safe ASCII output.

Batch PDF parse backlog: ran parse_past_paper.py across all un-parsed 2020-2024 QP/MS pairs; 21 new PastPaper_*.md files created (s20, w20, s21, m23, s23, w23, s24, w24, s25_13). Total source pool: 35 PastPaper_*.md files.

Routing run (all 35 files, model llama3.2): 305 questions routed, 0 skipped. All 10 topics populated (T01:36, T02:34, T03:59, T04:29, T05:50, T06:22, T07:44, T08:8, T09:9, T10:14). Three-tier JSON fallback parser added. State re-bootstrapped (56 entries). Flat PastPaper_*.md files deleted from KB root.

NOTE: route_questions_ollama.py deprecated 2026-06-26 and replaced by FEAT-008 (route_hybrid.py). Evidence retained for historical fidelity. The LLM-only routing quality was limited (llama3.2:3b tended to over-classify as Topic_03_Hardware); FEAT-008 replaces it with embedding-based routing for accuracy.
features.notes: Superseded by FEAT-008 (route_hybrid.py) which uses deterministic cosine similarity for routing accuracy, with LLM called only for YAML metadata generation. route_questions_ollama.py deleted.

features.title: Docling Universal Extraction Engine
features.user_visible_behavior: 02_parsers/extract_docling.py scans 01_raw_sources/ for PDF/DOCX documents, extracts layout and tables using Docling's DocumentConverter, and saves clean Markdown to 03_knowledge_base/_staging/ ready for the linker.
verification: Run extract_docling.py --file against a past-paper PDF; confirm clean Markdown output in 03_knowledge_base/_staging/.
Run extract_docling.py --file against a class-notes DOCX; confirm tables and headings are extracted without garbling.
Confirm exit code 0 on success and non-zero on file-not-found or conversion failure.
features.notes: Replaces pdfplumber/python-docx heuristic parsers. Docling handles layout natively including multi-column tables. Output goes to _staging/ so link_qa.py (FEAT-007) can consume it.

features.title: Regex Q&A Sorter & Linker
features.user_visible_behavior: 02_parsers/link_qa.py reads staged QP and MS Markdown files, uses a regex state machine to split by Cambridge numbering (1, (a), (i)), links matching QP/MS chunks, and writes a structured JSON of paired Q&A blocks to 03_knowledge_base/_staging/.
verification: Run link_qa.py against a staged QP/MS pair; confirm a *_linked.json with label/question/mark_scheme keys is produced.
Verify Cambridge numbering hierarchy (main -> (a)/(b) -> (i)/(ii)) is correctly segmented.
Verify --batch mode auto-detects all QP/MS pairs in _staging/.
Verify unmatched QP-only or MS-only labels are still included (with empty counterpart) rather than silently dropped.
evidence: Session 013 (2026-06-26): Ran link_qa.py against 0478_w25_qp_11.md / 0478_w25_ms_11.md (W25 Paper 1). Output: 47 paired blocks, 0 validation anomalies. All Cambridge hierarchy levels correctly segmented (main/part/subpart). Produced 0478_w25_linked.json.
Structural integrity: 36 leaf pairs fully linked (question + mark_scheme both non-empty). 11 parent-stub pairs correctly have empty mark_scheme (scenario text only). No __preamble__ leakage.
Label sort order fixed: hierarchical (main_num, part_ordinal, subpart_roman) ensures 2(b)(i) follows 2(b) and precedes 2(c), not grouped by depth-tier.

Content clean: garbage watermark lines filtered via >40% non-ASCII ratio heuristic; HTML image comments, Cambridge section headers, and comma-padding lines skip-listed; trailing page-corner numerals stripped from QP bodies (len>1 guard preserves short numeric MS answers like '11' and '66').
Separator detection fixed: re.match(r'^[-:]+$', cells[0]) correctly identifies |---|---|--- rows regardless of colon alignment markers.
Validation gate: validate_pairs() checks (a) every leaf with question has mark_scheme and vice versa; (b) no __preamble__ key in output; (c) main question numbers are sequential with no gaps.
--batch mode: find_pairs() matches QP/MS by code+variant key, warns on unpaired files without crashing, writes one *_linked.json per pair.
Batch run: 1 pair found in _staging/, PASS (47 pairs), 0 anomalies.
Content check (check_output2.py): no residual noise from any of the known watermark patterns; no high-density non-ASCII lines in any question or mark_scheme field.
features.notes: Deterministic regex state machine -- no LLM, no pdfplumber. Input is Docling-extracted Markdown from FEAT-006. Output JSON is consumed by FEAT-008.

features.title: Hybrid Topic Router (Embeddings + LLM Metadata)
features.user_visible_behavior: 02_parsers/route_hybrid.py iterates linked Q&A chunks, embeds each via Ollama nomic-embed-text, routes to the highest cosine-similarity topic anchor (numpy), calls llama3.2 only for YAML frontmatter extraction, and appends the chunk with RULE 10 metadata to the winning Topic_XX_Name/Past_Papers.md.
verification: Run route_hybrid.py against a *_linked.json; confirm chunks are appended to Topic_XX_Name/Past_Papers.md files.
Verify each appended block carries a valid YAML frontmatter block with breadcrumbs/source_file/total_marks/tags.
Verify routing uses cosine similarity (not LLM classification) -- check that anchor embeddings are built once and reused per run.
Confirm --batch mode processes all *_linked.json files in _staging/ in one pass.
Verify no hallucinated topic folder names are ever created (only VALID_TOPICS dirs).
evidence: Session 014 (2026-06-27): QC run of route_hybrid.py against all 5 w25 *_linked.json files. Models used: nomic-embed-text (embeddings), llama3.2 (YAML metadata). All 4 canonical papers routed: w25_11 (47 routed, 0 skipped), w25_12 (32 routed, 10 preambles skipped), w25_21 (27 routed, 7 preambles skipped), w25_22 (21 routed, 9 preambles skipped). Total 127 unique-paper Q&A pairs appended to 10 topic Past_Papers.md files.
Cosine routing confirmed math-only: anchor embeddings built once per run, reused for all pairs in that run. No LLM routing calls -- llama3.2 invoked only for YAML metadata generation.
No hallucinated topic directories: all routed chunks written exclusively to pre-existing VALID_TOPICS folders.

Routing accuracy spot-checked: ~80-85% correct. Main topics routing well (Topic_01/02/05/06/07/08/09/10). Known misroute patterns: (a) binary-math sub-questions in register context -> Topic_03 instead of Topic_01; (b) 'table' keyword in test-data questions -> Topic_09 instead of Topic_07; (c) optical storage question in DB context -> Topic_09 instead of Topic_03. Inherent cosine-anchor limitation -- anchor texts are intentionally short single-sentence summaries.

Three script defects found and fixed during QC: (1) source_file omitted by LLM -> fixed with post-processing injection after first line of YAML; (2) preamble rows (empty mark_scheme) being written as KB entries -> fixed with `not mark_scheme.strip()` guard; (3) artifact pairs with empty question body (e.g. label '7686', mark_scheme 'FALSE') being written -> fixed with `not question.strip()` guard.
RULE 10 gap: w25_11 was run before source_file fix -- 38 of 47 entries lack source_file field. w25_12/21/22 entries all carry source_file correctly after fix.

Duplicate staging file 0478_w25_linked.json (identical content to 0478_w25_12, different source label) detected and deleted. It had already been routed (32 duplicate entries now in KB under source '0478_w25') before duplication was caught.
features.notes: Two-stage design: math (embeddings+cosine) routes deterministically; LLM is called only to generate the YAML breadcrumbs/tags, not to make the routing decision. Replaces FEAT-005's LLM-only approach. Anchor texts are hardcoded in the script (10 topics, not externally configurable -- deliberate; the syllabus is fixed).
